compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Why
With many attestations every consumer must address a specific kind, without breaking the existing single-verdict API/CLI/MCP/schema.
## What
`verify_signature(ticket_id, kind="plan-review")` ALWAYS returns a SINGLE verdict dict
(schema `verify_signature_result.schema.json` UNCHANGED); `kind` defaults to `"plan-review"`
so existing no-kind callers keep back-compat. Internally it reads
`state["attestations"][kind]`, falling back to the legacy `state["signature"]` mirror when
the map is absent. Add a SEPARATE `verify_attestations(ticket_id) -> {kind: verdict}`
entry-point for the verify-all map (NOT a new return shape of `verify_signature`). CLI:
`--kind K` = single-gate exit code; no `--kind` = human-readable listing via
`verify_attestations` (exit 0 iff all certified). MCP `verify_signature` stays single
(kind defaults to plan-review). SCOPE BOUNDARY: this story owns the `verify_signature` MCP
tool + its (unchanged) result schema; the `attestations` field in the ticket-state MCP
response is owned by ed26.
## Scope
`src/rebar/signing.py` (`verify_signature` add `kind=`, `verify_attestations`,
`verify_signature_cli`), `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` (verify tool only).
## Depends on
Requires 114f merged (reads `state["attestations"]`).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `verify_signature(ticket_id, kind="plan-review")` returns a single verdict; reads `attestations[kind]` with legacy `signature` fallback; schema unchanged.
- [ ] `verify_attestations(ticket_id)` returns `{kind: verdict}` over all present attestations.
- [ ] CLI `--kind K` exit 0 iff that kind certified; no `--kind` lists all, exit 0 iff all certified.
- [ ] Existing no-kind library/MCP callers keep single-verdict back-compat (default kind plan-review).
- [ ] Legacy single-signature ticket verifies under its derived kind.

## Clarification (impl): no-kind = mirror (back-compat), not list-all
- `verify_signature(ticket_id, kind=None)` verifies the most-recent `signature` MIRROR
  (exact pre-attestations behavior) so every existing no-kind caller + the single-verdict
  JSON/CLI contract is preserved; an explicit kind verifies that kind STRICTLY from the map.
- CLI `verify-signature <t>` (no --kind) returns the legacy SINGLE verdict; `--kind K`
  verifies kind K (single verdict, exit 0 iff certified — the CI-gate form). The full
  per-kind set is exposed via the library `verify_attestations` and the `attestations`
  field of `show` (ed26), NOT as a CLI map (which would break the single-verdict contract).
- [ ] no-kind library/CLI verify is byte-compatible with prior single-verdict behavior; verify_attestations(ticket_id) returns the {kind: verdict} map for programmatic all-kinds.
compiled_state.title: Verification surface: per-kind verify + verify-all map
